title: Music Festival Noise Plans Worry People
summary: People living and working near a big music festival in Cornwall are worried about plans to make the music louder. The festival, called Boardmasters, happens in Newquay for five days in August. Organizers want to increase the noise levels and play music later at night. They say this will help the festival stay popular and fun. But hotel owners, local leaders, and residents say louder music will disturb people and businesses. They think the festival should plan better instead of making more noise. A council will decide on the plans soon.

background_read: Boardmasters is a large music festival held every year in Newquay, Cornwall. It attracts many visitors and features live performances. Noise levels are measured in decibels, and higher decibels mean louder sounds. Local councils often set rules for events to protect residents from too much noise. This article talks about a disagreement between festival organizers who want to increase noise and local people who are concerned about disturbance.
Article_Structure: WHO: Festival organizers, residents, hotel owners, and local council members
WHAT: Plans to increase noise levels at the Boardmasters festival
WHY: Organizers want to stay competitive, but others worry about disturbance

perspectives.description: They believe increasing noise will help the festival stay fun and popular.
They think louder music will disturb sleep and harm businesses.
